blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Can I tell you what I actually think? Not the version that protects your feelings — the version that's true.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I think you've been protecting the thing you have with him by keeping this separate from it. I understand why. But I think the longer it stays separate, the more the separation becomes the thing itself — the hidden thing starts defining what you are together more than what you actually share. And I think you know that. I think that's why you're sitting here.
Cassandra looked at her.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: If it's real — what you have with him — the truth won't break it. It might change its shape. But the kind of real that breaks at the first difficult thing wasn't going to hold anyway.
She met Cassandra's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: But hiding it will break it. Not all at once. Gradually. The way things do when there's something underneath that nobody names.
The kitchen was quiet. Outside, a sparrow landed on the windowsill and then left.
Cassandra looked at her hands around the mug.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I keep thinking about what he said. On the porch. That he's not going anywhere. That he just wants to understand what matters.
She looked up.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: He already suspects something. He's just waiting for me to be ready.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: And?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: And I think I am.
She said it slowly, testing the weight of it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I think I've been ready for a while and I've been calling it something else.
Lena nodded.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Then go. Not today. Not this second. But soon.
A pause.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Don't let being almost ready become its own way of waiting.
For the first time in weeks, the thing she'd been carrying felt like something with a shape — something with an edge, something that could be set down.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Tomorrow. I'm going to ask him to meet me tomorrow.
